title: oxalis riparia
intro: Oxalis riparia, also known as the water violet or purple wood sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to North America and is characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Oxalis riparia can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system.
